who i am

About

Graduate of a Professional Master's in Software Engineering and New Technologies (ISET Sfax), I design full-stack applications with a strong preference for backend systems and AI-driven features.

My final-year project is a reading and writing assistance platform for people with dyslexia: real-time text prediction, contextual AI correction, and speech synthesis — a project at the crossroads of software engineering and accessibility.

I also worked in London on a speech recognition system for automatic meeting transcription, deployed on Azure. Based in Sousse, Tunisia, and currently looking for a role in backend development or applied AI.

career path

Experience

January — June 2026

Final-Year Internship — Reading & Writing Assistance Platform

TSI-Network
  • Real-time writing assistance with word/sentence prediction — NestJS/React architecture communicating over WebSocket (Socket.io) with local AI models
  • Contextual spelling and grammar correction module (Ollama, Phi-3, Qwen) with non-intrusive suggestions
  • Phonological support and voice reading (faster-whisper transcription, Piper synthesis), progress tracking via PostgreSQL
February — June 2024

Internship — Artificial Intelligence Engineering

Caustaza Group LTD · London, Covent Garden
  • Speech recognition system (Python, Machine Learning) for automatic meeting transcription
  • Integration of Azure OpenAI and NLP to generate accurate meeting summaries
  • Deployment on Microsoft Azure (Docker, FastAPI)
selected work

Projects

accessibilitydyslexia

Reading & Writing Assistance Platform for Dyslexia

A communication assistance app for people with dyslexia: real-time word/sentence prediction, contextual AI-based spelling and grammar correction, voice reading, and an institution dashboard for tracking students.
